title: alchemilla arvensis subsp. cornucopioides
intro: Alchemilla arvensis subsp. cornucopioides, also known as the Lady's Mantle or common lady's mantle, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its rosette of leaves and clusters of small, star-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, diarrhea, and menstrual disorders. The Lady's Mantle is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing menstrual cramps, relieving anxiety and stress, and supporting liver and kidney function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Lady's Mantle can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, or as a dietary supplement to promote overall well-being.
